name: HLA-E/NKG2A Senescence Checkpoint Blockade
summary: Conceptual immune-checkpoint strategy based on blocking HLA-E-mediated inhibition of NK and CD8 T-cell responses against senescent cells.
mechanism_summary: Aims to lift inhibitory HLA-E/NKG2A signaling that suppresses NK-cell and CD8 T-cell responses to senescent cells, based on human cell and tissue evidence.

open_questions: Whether checkpoint blockade can selectively enhance senescent-cell clearance in vivo
Whether aging tissues tolerate increased NK and CD8 T-cell activity
Which biomarkers would distinguish beneficial clearance from generalized inflammation
